scope.context_note: Volume III section from an undergraduate real analysis note set. The local mathematical setting is chapter 'Continuity', section 'Limits at Infinity'. The audit goal is in-depth learning of the structure: direct dependencies should be concepts needed to state each node precisely, with common ambient structure carried by the nearest appropriate ancestor.

review_instructions.order: Nodes are topologically sorted so dependencies and ambient ancestors appear before their users when possible.
review_instructions.state: Use reorder/state/working-delta.json as cumulative accepted state across all batches.
review_instructions.checker: Run reorder/tools/check_dependency_edge.py before accepting any add/remove.

nodes.title: Infinite Adherent Points
nodes.statement_display:
Let $X\subseteq\mathbb{R}$. The point $+\infty$ is adherent to $X$ if
and only if for every $M\in\mathbb{R}$ there exists $x\in X$ such that
$x>M$. The point $-\infty$ is adherent to $X$ if and only if for every
$M\in\mathbb{R}$ there exists $x\in X$ such that $x<M$.
nodes.statement_tex:
Let $X\subseteq\mathbb{R}$. The point $+\infty$ is adherent to $X$ if
and only if for every $M\in\mathbb{R}$ there exists $x\in X$ such that
$x>M$. The point $-\infty$ is adherent to $X$ if and only if for every
$M\in\mathbb{R}$ there exists $x\in X$ such that $x<M$.

nodes.title: Limit at Infinity
nodes.statement_display:
Let $X \subseteq \mathbb{R}$ be such that for every $M \in \mathbb{R}$ there exists $x \in X$ with $x > M$, let $f : X \to \mathbb{R}$, and let $L \in \mathbb{R}$. The limit of $f$ at $+\infty$ equals $L$ if and only if
for every $\varepsilon > 0$ there exists $M \in \mathbb{R}$ such that for every $x \in X$, $x > M$ implies $|f(x) - L| < \varepsilon$.
nodes.statement_tex:
Let $X \subseteq \mathbb{R}$ be such that for every $M \in \mathbb{R}$ there exists $x \in X$ with $x > M$, let $f : X \to \mathbb{R}$, and let $L \in \mathbb{R}$. The limit of $f$ at $+\infty$ equals $L$ if and only if
for every $\varepsilon > 0$ there exists $M \in \mathbb{R}$ such that for every $x \in X$, $x > M$ implies $|f(x) - L| < \varepsilon$.
